name: Booking.com
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Booking.com — the Amsterdam-based travel-booking behemoth whose brand discipline is the opposite of luxury hospitality: dense, data-rich, urgency-driven, and unapologetically transactional. Canvas is pure white with crisp ink. The signature is the Booking.com blue (#003580) used at full saturation on the navigation, on the primary 'Search' CTA, and on the wordmark — a deep cobalt that reads as 'trustworthy financial institution' more than 'travel'. Type runs a precise grotesk (Booking Sans / Roboto / system grotesk) at modest scale. The brand voltage is the urgency overlay — '5 people are looking at this property', 'Last booked 2 hours ago' — micro-copy interventions that turn search results into a high-pressure auction floor.

design_language.motion_signature: Urgency-driven. 'Booked X hours ago' counters update in real-time via subtle 200ms cross-fade. 'Limited rooms' badges pulse red over a 1.2s ease-in-out loop. Hover on property tile fades a 1px blue border in over 150ms. The brand voice is 'transactional pressure', never 'leisurely browse'.

trademark_signals: Booking.com blue (#003580) — a deep cobalt that reads as 'banking' rather than 'travel' — used at full saturation on every nav bar, every primary CTA, and the wordmark itself
Yellow secondary accent (#febb02) — used on the 'Genius' loyalty-program badge, on the 'Booking value' score sticker, and on the Genius tier-circles — never as a primary CTA fill
Urgency overlay micro-copy — 'Last booked 4 hours ago', 'Only 2 rooms left at this price', '17 people are looking right now' — overlaid on every property card
10-out-of-10 review-score lozenge — the iconic Booking review-score badge with the number, the 'Excellent' label, and the review count — appears on every property hero
Dense property-result grid — 4-up at desktop with map, list, filters, and pagination all visible simultaneously — refusing the 'curated 3-hero' convention of luxury booking sites
ai_slop_avoided: Don't drop the urgency micro-copy. The 'X people are looking' overlays ARE the brand's voltage — without them, Booking reads as a generic travel site
Don't soften the cobalt blue. The deep #003580 is the trust-anchor color; lightening to a friendlier blue reads as 'travel boutique' off-brand
Don't curate the result grid. The brand reads as dense — 20+ properties visible above the fold; 3-hero curation reads as luxury, not Booking
Don't hide the review-score lozenge. The 10-out-of-10 badge with the 'Excellent' label is the most copyable Booking asset — never make it small
Don't paint accent moments yellow. The yellow is reserved for Genius loyalty and review-value stickers — using it on CTAs reads as off-brand
